primary_meaning: to bring something back to your mind
secondary_meaning: to not forget to do something
usage_notes: Used when talking about past events or reminding oneself
common_collocations: remember something, remember to do, remember when
image_search_query: person thinking trying to remember something
image_context: A person with a thoughtful expression, hand on chin, looking upward as if trying to recall something
example_1: I remember the first day I came here.
example_1_translation: Eu lembro do primeiro dia em que vim aqui.
example_2: Remember to lock the door before you leave.
example_2_translation: Lembre-se de trancar a porta antes de sair.
example_3: She could not remember his name.
example_3_translation: Ela não conseguia lembrar o nome dele.
pronunciation_tips: Emphasize the second syllable: re-MEM-ber
memory_hook: Re-member - put the pieces back together in your mind

primary_meaning: to not be able to remember something
secondary_meaning: to leave something somewhere by accident
usage_notes: Opposite of 'remember'. Past tense is 'forgot', past participle 'forgotten'
common_collocations: forget something, forget to do, almost forget
image_search_query: person looking confused trying to remember something
image_context: A person looking frustrated and confused, opening arms in a gesture of not knowing
example_1: I forgot her birthday last year.
example_1_translation: Eu esqueci do aniversário dela ano passado.
example_2: Don't forget to call me when you arrive.
example_2_translation: Não esqueça de me ligar quando você chegar.
example_3: He forgot his keys at the office.
example_3_translation: Ele esqueceu as chaves no escritório.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: for-GET. The 'for' is weak, 'get' is strong
memory_hook: For-get - to get something out of your mind

primary_meaning: to think something is true
secondary_meaning: to have faith or trust in someone
usage_notes: Used with 'in' for religious faith: believe in God
common_collocations: believe in, believe that, believe me
image_search_query: person with hands together praying or hoping
image_context: A person standing with closed eyes, hands clasped together, expressing faith and belief
example_1: I believe what you are saying is true.
example_1_translation: Eu acredito que o que você está dizendo é verdade.
example_2: She believes in hard work.
example_2_translation: Ela acredita no trabalho duro.
example_3: Do you believe in ghosts?
example_3_translation: Você acredita em fantasmas?
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: be-LIE-ve. The 'lie' part has a long 'i' sound
memory_hook: Be-lie-ve - to have a 'lie' (statement) you 'be' hold as true

primary_meaning: to make a choice between two or more options
usage_notes: Often followed by 'to' + verb or 'that' clause
common_collocations: decide to do, decide on, decide between
image_search_query: person at crossroads looking thoughtful
image_context: A person standing at a crossroads, looking thoughtful with a road sign pointing different directions
example_1: We decided to go to the beach this weekend.
example_1_translation: Nós decidimos ir à praia neste fim de semana.
example_2: She could not decide which dress to buy.
example_2_translation: Ela não conseguia decidir qual vestido comprar.
example_3: It's hard to decide between the two options.
example_3_translation: É difícil decidir entre as duas opções.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: de-CIDE. Emphasize 'cide'
memory_hook: De-cide - to cut away (cis/cide) the doubts and choose

primary_meaning: to want something to happen and think it is possible
usage_notes: Often used with 'for' + noun or 'to' + verb
common_collocations: hope for, hope to do, I hope so, I hope not
image_search_query: person looking at stars with hopeful expression
image_context: A person looking up at a starry night sky with a gentle smile and hopeful eyes
example_1: I hope it doesn't rain tomorrow.
example_1_translation: Espero que não chova amanhã.
example_2: She hopes to get the job.
example_2_translation: Ela espera conseguir o emprego.
example_3: We are hoping for good news.
example_3_translation: Estamos esperando boas notícias.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable, like 'rope' without the 'r'. Long 'o' sound
memory_hook: Hope - like holding a 'rope' of optimism

primary_meaning: to feel anxious or nervous about something
usage_notes: Often used with 'about' + noun or 'that' clause
common_collocations: worry about, don't worry, stop worrying
image_search_query: person looking stressed with hands on head
image_context: A person with a stressed expression, hands on their head, showing worry and anxiety
example_1: Don't worry about me, I'm fine.
example_1_translation: Não se preocupe comigo, eu estou bem.
example_2: She worries too much about her exams.
example_2_translation: Ela se preocupa demais com as provas.
example_3: His health worries me.
example_3_translation: A saúde dele me preocupa.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: WOR-ry. Emphasize the first syllable
memory_hook: Wor-ry - like getting 'war' in your mind

primary_meaning: to experience an emotion or physical sensation
secondary_meaning: to touch something with your hands
usage_notes: Link verb for emotions: feel happy, feel tired. Past tense: felt
common_collocations: feel like, feel that, feel happy, feel cold
image_search_query: person touching their heart with eyes closed
image_context: A person with one hand on their heart and eyes closed, expressing deep feeling and emotion
example_1: I feel happy when I'm with my friends.
example_1_translation: Eu me sinto feliz quando estou com meus amigos.
example_2: The water feels very cold.
example_2_translation: A água está muito fria ao toque.
example_3: She feels like going out tonight.
example_3_translation: Ela está com vontade de sair esta noite.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable, long 'ee' sound, like 'wheel' without the 'w'
memory_hook: Feel - to 'feel' with your heart and hands

primary_meaning: very happy and looking forward to something
usage_notes: Often used with 'about' or 'to' + verb. Not used before noun directly
common_collocations: excited about, excited to do, get excited
image_search_query: person jumping with excitement hands raised
image_context: A person with arms raised in joy, jumping with excitement, big smile on their face
example_1: I'm so excited about my birthday next week.
example_1_translation: Estou tão animado com o meu aniversário na próxima semana.
example_2: The children were excited to open their presents.
example_2_translation: As crianças estavam animadas para abrir os presentes.
example_3: She got excited when she heard the news.
example_3_translation: Ela ficou animada quando ouviu a notícia.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: ex-CI-ted. Emphasize 'ci'
memory_hook: Ex-cited - to be 'cited' (called out) with joy out of normal state

primary_meaning: tired and uninterested because nothing exciting is happening
secondary_meaning: feeling that time is passing slowly
usage_notes: Often used with 'with' or 'by'. 'Boring' describes the thing that makes you bored
common_collocations: bored with, bored by, get bored, so bored
image_search_query: person lying on couch looking bored and tired
image_context: A person lying on a couch, looking at the ceiling with a bored expression, arms hanging down
example_1: I'm bored with this TV show.
example_1_translation: Estou entediado com este programa de TV.
example_2: The lecture was so boring that everyone slept.
example_2_translation: A palestra era tão entediante que todos dormiram.
example_3: He never gets bored.
example_3_translation: Ele nunca se entedia.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable, rhymes with 'door' and 'more'
memory_hook: Bo-red - like being 'born' into a dull world

primary_meaning: needing rest or sleep
secondary_meaning: feeling that you have done too much of something
usage_notes: Use 'tired' for physical tiredness, 'bored' for mental uninterested state
common_collocations: tired of, tired from, so tired, feel tired
image_search_query: person rubbing eyes showing tiredness
image_context: A person rubbing their eyes with a yawn, showing they are very tired and sleepy
example_1: I'm too tired to go out tonight.
example_1_translation: Estou cansado demais para sair esta noite.
example_2: She was tired after working all day.
example_2_translation: Ela estava cansada depois de trabalhar o dia todo.
example_3: I'm tired of waiting for you.
example_3_translation: Estou cansado de esperar por você.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: TI-red. Emphasize the first syllable
memory_hook: Tired - like your 'tire' (energy) is empty
common_mistakes: 'Tired' not 'tired' - sounds like 'fired' but starts with 't'

primary_meaning: feeling fear or worry about something bad that might happen
usage_notes: Often used with 'of' or 'that' clause. 'I'm afraid' is also used to apologize politely
common_collocations: afraid of, afraid to do, I'm afraid so, I'm afraid not
image_search_query: person cowering in fear hiding behind hands
image_context: A person crouching down, hands covering their face, showing fear and terror
example_1: She is afraid of spiders.
example_1_translation: Ela tem medo de aranhas.
example_2: He was afraid to tell her the truth.
example_2_translation: Ele tinha medo de contar a verdade para ela.
example_3: I'm afraid that something bad will happen.
example_3_translation: Temo que algo ruim vai acontecer.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: a-FRAID. Emphasize the second syllable
memory_hook: A-fraid - after 'a' fright, you feel 'a-fraid'

primary_meaning: not easy to do or understand
usage_notes: Opposite is 'easy'. Can describe tasks, concepts, or situations
common_collocations: difficult to do, difficult for, find difficult
image_search_query: person struggling with puzzle or problem
image_context: A person looking frustrated, scratching their head in front of a complex puzzle
example_1: Math is very difficult for me.
example_1_translation: Matemática é muito difícil para mim.
example_2: This exercise is difficult to understand.
example_2_translation: Este exercício é difícil de entender.
example_3: She found it difficult to make a decision.
example_3_translation: Ela achou difícil tomar uma decisão.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: DIF-fi-cul-t. Emphasize the first syllable
memory_hook: Dif-fi-cult - 'ficult' sounds like 'actual' - it IS actually difficult

primary_meaning: not the same as something else
secondary_meaning: unusual or special in some way
usage_notes: Use 'from' in British English, 'than' in American English, 'to' also common
common_collocations: different from, different than, look different, totally different
image_search_query: two different colored objects side by side
image_context: Two objects of completely different colors, shapes and sizes placed next to each other
example_1: My house is different from yours.
example_1_translation: A minha casa é diferente da sua.
example_2: This tastes different than I expected.
example_2_translation: Isso tem um gosto diferente do que eu esperava.
example_3: Everyone has a different opinion.
example_3_translation: Cada pessoa tem uma opinião diferente.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: dif-fer-ent. Emphasize the first syllable
memory_hook: Dif-fer-ent - each 'fer' makes it 'different'

primary_meaning: having great value or significance
usage_notes: One of the most common adjectives in English. Often used in academic and professional contexts
common_collocations: important to, important for, most important, it's important that
image_search_query: golden star or trophy representing importance
image_context: A large golden star or trophy on a pedestal, surrounded by a spotlight, representing something important
example_1: Education is very important for children.
example_1_translation: Educação é muito importante para as crianças.
example_2: It's important that you understand this.
example_2_translation: É importante que você entenda isso.
example_3: She is an important person in the company.
example_3_translation: Ela é uma pessoa importante na empresa.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: im-POR-tant. Emphasize the second syllable
memory_hook: Im-por-tant - you 'port' value 'in' - it's important

primary_meaning: catching and holding your attention
secondary_meaning: making you curious to know more
usage_notes: Opposite is 'boring'. Often used to describe books, films, stories, and people
common_collocations: interesting to, interesting for, look interesting, sound interesting
image_search_query: person reading a book with engaged expression
image_context: A person sitting in a comfortable chair, reading a book with a focused and interested expression
example_1: This book is very interesting.
example_1_translation: Este livro é muito interessante.
example_2: He told us an interesting story about his trip.
example_2_translation: Ele nos contou uma história interessante sobre a viagem dele.
example_3: What's the most interesting part?
example_3_translation: Qual é a parte mais interessante?
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: IN-ter-est-ing. Emphasize 'ter'
memory_hook: In-ter-est-ing - you're 'in' the 'terest' (test) and it's good

primary_meaning: able or likely to cause harm or danger
usage_notes: Used to describe situations, activities, substances, or behaviors that can cause harm
common_collocations: dangerous to, dangerous for, very dangerous, potentially dangerous
image_search_query: warning sign with danger symbol
image_context: A bright yellow warning sign with an exclamation mark and skull symbol, indicating danger ahead
example_1: Swimming in this river can be dangerous.
example_1_translation: Nadar neste rio pode ser perigoso.
example_2: It's dangerous to drive without a seatbelt.
example_2_translation: É perigoso dirigir sem cinto de segurança.
example_3: This area is dangerous at night.
example_3_translation: Esta área é perigosa à noite.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: DANG-er-ous. Emphasize the first syllable
memory_hook: Dan-ger-ous - there's 'danger' in it and it's 'ous' (too much)

primary_meaning: able to be done or achieved
secondary_meaning: that may or may not happen
usage_notes: Opposite is 'impossible'. Often used with 'it is possible to' or 'as ... as possible'
common_collocations: possible to, as soon as possible, if possible, make it possible
image_search_query: lightbulb showing idea or possibility
image_context: A glowing lightbulb held in a hand, representing an idea or possibility being born
example_1: Is it possible to finish this today?
example_1_translation: É possível terminar isso hoje?
example_2: Come as soon as possible.
example_2_translation: Venha o mais rápido possível.
example_3: Anything is possible if you try hard enough.
example_3_translation: Qualquer coisa é possível se você se esforçar o suficiente.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: POS-si-ble. Emphasize the first syllable
memory_hook: Pos-si-ble - 'pos' (put) it together and it's 'sible' (able)

primary_meaning: needed to achieve a particular result
usage_notes: Often used with 'to' + verb or 'for' + noun. 'It's necessary that' uses subjunctive
common_collocations: necessary to, necessary for, if necessary, absolutely necessary
image_search_query: checklist with important items marked
image_context: A checklist on paper with several items marked with a red checkmark, showing necessary things to do
example_1: Sleep is necessary for good health.
example_1_translation: Sono é necessário para uma boa saúde.
example_2: It's necessary to bring your passport.
example_2_translation: É necessário trazer o passaporte.
example_3: If necessary, call the police.
example_3_translation: Se necessário, chame a polícia.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: NESS-es-sar-y. Emphasize the first syllable
memory_hook: Ne-ces-sa-ry - you 'cess' (stop) without it - it's necessary

primary_meaning: complete or entire, with nothing missing
usage_notes: Often used with 'the' + noun. Can come before or after the noun
common_collocations: the whole, whole lot, whole day, whole family
image_search_query: full complete pie vs sliced pie
image_context: A complete unbroken pie on the left, and the same pie sliced on the right, showing the contrast
example_1: She ate the whole cake by herself.
example_1_translation: Ela comeu o bolo inteiro sozinha.
example_2: I spent the whole day studying.
example_2_translation: Eu passei o dia inteiro estudando.
example_3: The whole family came to the party.
example_3_translation: A família inteira veio para a festa.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable, rhymes with 'goal' and 'bowl'
memory_hook: Whole - 'hole' with a 'w' - complete with nothing 'whole' missing
common_mistakes: 'Whole' not 'hole' - 'whole' means complete, 'hole' is a gap

primary_meaning: the cause or explanation for something
usage_notes: Often used with 'for' + noun or 'why' clause. 'The reason is that...'
common_collocations: reason for, reason why, the reason is, for some reason
image_search_query: question mark and lightbulb showing thinking
image_context: A bright lightbulb above a question mark, representing the reason and logic behind something
example_1: What's the reason for your absence?
example_1_translation: Qual é o motivo da sua ausência?
example_2: The reason I'm late is that my car broke down.
example_2_translation: O motivo pelo qual estou atrasado é que meu carro quebrou.
example_3: For some reason, I don't trust him.
example_3_translation: Por algum motivo, eu não confio nele.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: REA-son. Emphasize the first syllable, long 'ea' sound
memory_hook: Rea-son - think 'reason' with your 'rea' (real) mind

primary_meaning: a situation that is difficult to deal with
usage_notes: Common in both everyday and academic contexts. 'No problem' is casual response
common_collocations: problem with, solve a problem, have a problem, big problem
image_search_query: person scratching head in front of puzzle
image_context: A person with their head in their hands, looking at a complex puzzle they cannot solve
example_1: We have a problem with the computer.
example_1_translation: Temos um problema com o computador.
example_2: Can you solve this math problem?
example_2_translation: Você consegue resolver este problema de matemática?
example_3: No problem! I can help you.
example_3_translation: Sem problema! Eu posso te ajudar.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: PRO-blem. Emphasize the first syllable
memory_hook: Pro-blem - 'pro' (for) + 'blem' (blame) - something to fix

primary_meaning: a thought or plan that you have in your mind
usage_notes: One of the most common A2 words. 'That's a good idea' is frequent expression
common_collocations: good idea, have an idea, great idea, no idea
image_search_query: lightbulb appearing above person's head
image_context: A cartoon lightbulb lighting up above a person's head, representing a new idea
example_1: That's a great idea!
example_1_translation: Isso é uma ótima ideia!
example_2: I have no idea what happened.
example_2_translation: Não tenho ideia do que aconteceu.
example_3: Can you give me an idea of the price?
example_3_translation: Você pode me dar uma ideia do preço?
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: i-DE-a. Emphasize the second syllable
memory_hook: I-dea - 'I' see the 'dea' (dear) in my mind

primary_meaning: knowledge or skill gained from doing something
secondary_meaning: an event or activity that affects you
usage_notes: Can be countable (specific events) or uncountable (general knowledge)
common_collocations: work experience, life experience, previous experience, interesting experience
image_search_query: person with backpack traveling having experiences
image_context: A person hiking up a mountain with a backpack, experiencing the world and gaining experience
example_1: She has five years of experience in teaching.
example_1_translation: Ela tem cinco anos de experiência em ensino.
example_2: It was an amazing experience to meet her.
example_2_translation: Foi uma experiência incrível conhecê-la.
example_3: Learn from your mistakes.
example_3_translation: Aprenda com suas experiências.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: ex-PE-ri-ence. Emphasize 'pe'
memory_hook: Ex-pe-ri-ence - you 'ex' (out) 'peri' (through) and gain 'ence' (essence)

primary_meaning: the achievement of an aim or purpose
secondary_meaning: a person or thing that achieves what it aims to do
usage_notes: Can be uncountable for general concept, countable for specific achievements
common_collocations: success in, great success, success story, wish you success
image_search_query: trophy or medal representing success
image_context: A golden trophy on a pedestal with confetti falling around it, representing great success
example_1: Hard work is the key to success.
example_1_translation: Trabalho duro é a chave para o sucesso.
example_2: The party was a great success.
example_2_translation: A festa foi um grande sucesso.
example_3: I wish you success in your new job.
example_3_translation: Desejo-lhe sucesso no seu novo emprego.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: suc-CESS. Emphasize the second syllable
memory_hook: Suc-cess - you 'suc' (such) 'cess' (cession) - achieve great things

primary_meaning: an unpleasant emotion caused by danger or pain
usage_notes: Often used with 'of' + noun or 'that' clause. 'For fear of' means 'to avoid'
common_collocations: fear of, fear that, in fear, without fear
image_search_query: person in dark forest looking scared
image_context: A person standing alone in a dark, foggy forest, looking around with fear in their eyes
example_1: She has a fear of heights.
example_1_translation: Ela tem medo de altura.
example_2: His fear was written on his face.
example_2_translation: O medo estava escrito no rosto dele.
example_3: He did it without fear.
example_3_translation: Ele fez isso sem medo.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable, rhymes with 'deer' and 'near'
memory_hook: Fear - when you 'hear' danger, you feel fear
